situations.TextKey: A cabinet of blackened oak sorts papers by guilt, not alphabet. One drawer opens with a sound like a verdict being reconsidered.

value.Purpose: remove a card from your deck

situations.TextKey: A card disappears into the wrong folder and returns wearing a different title. A duplicate notice follows it out.
The drawer gives up one obsolete procedure, but bites down on your patience.
A brass drawer offers certified copies of anything placed inside. The copies are perfect. The drawer is hungry.
The drawer duplicates the procedure with impeccable authority. It also issues a duplicate notice.
Something official and useful comes loose from the drawer. The drawer keeps a painful filing fee.
A slate fee table updates while you read it. Each new line looks older and more legally binding than the last.
The table accepts the ruinous sum and stamps two procedures as properly improved.
The table discovers that the city owes you an old administrative refund.
Behind a dusty desk lie lost seals, receipt spikes, wax knives, and forms nobody wants to admit they misplaced.

value.Purpose: remove a card from your deck
remove a card from your deck

situations.TextKey: You give up one old procedure before the desk releases something useful.
One wrong thing leaves your pile. The desk charges you in bruised patience.
A vendor unfolds a counter covered in seals, receipts, and legally glowing merchandise. Every item has been licensed by someone who is currently denying responsibility.
The prices are official, which somehow makes them worse.
The vendor produces a relic from under the counter and writes your pain into the receipt.
A heavy ledger invites formal complaints. Its previous entries are complaining about the complaints below them.

value.Purpose: remove a card from your deck

situations.TextKey: One useless procedure leaves your file, but the ledger issues a duplicate notice.
The ledger writes something useful, then promises to make the next fight more stressful.
At the end of a bench sits a counter that trades lost waiting time for small official advantages.
The clerk hands you a relic and stamps fatigue onto the next appointment.
A procedure improves. A duplicate notice takes your former place.
A clerk looks up, understands almost everything, and reaches for a stamp. This is either a miracle or a trap.
The stamp fills you with temporary authority. It also files a duplicate notice.
The next opponents are sent to the wrong room first. Finding that out still hurts.
Three witnesses swear you were here before. Unfortunately, none of them agree on what you were doing.

value.Purpose: remove a card from your deck

situations.TextKey: The witness produces a relic and two duplicate notices. The other witnesses object.
One false statement leaves your file. The queue takes ten hit points of your patience.
A sealed side door claims to lead directly to the right corridor. The seal disagrees in very small print.
The next fight will be more informative, but the seal takes payment in blood.
The seal grants you a relic and warns the next opposition to take you seriously.
For one impossible minute, no one is responsible for anything. A cup of tea steams beside an abandoned ledger.
It tastes like dust, mercy, and exactly ten percent of your maximum health.
The notes contain one genuinely useful procedural correction.
A filing cabinet opens helpfully. The other furniture watches it with professional suspicion.

value.Purpose: remove a card from your deck

Choices.TextKey: Let it find a better form.

situations.TextKey: One unnecessary item is filed somewhere you will never have to visit.
One procedure is transformed into something more suitable, according to the cabinet.
An old receipt proves that you once nearly did everything correctly. The ink is faded, but the implication is useful.
A clerk grudgingly pays out an administrative refund.
The receipt proves prior effort and unlocks a useful option.
A wall map shows three routes to the same office. Two exist only on Tuesdays, and one is arguing with itself.
The route fills you with misplaced confidence and a duplicate notice.
The annotations are useful and terrifying in equal measure.
A narrow window opens into an archive older than the city ordinance. A hand made of dust offers two kinds of help.
The archive gives you a relic that still remembers how to be useful.
One of your procedures is copied with archival precision.
A sealed evidence crate, stamped in three colors of wax. Nobody has claimed it in decades.
The waiting room. The chairs are terrible, but nobody can reach you here.

Choices.TextKey: Wait it out (heal 25% of max HP)

gargoyle_chalk.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 9 Block.

red_ribbon_spool.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, apply 2 Paperwork to all enemies.

notary_candle.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 12 Block.

archive_lantern.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, apply 2 Doubt to all enemies.

clerk_badge.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 1 Max Energy.

stone_counterweight.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 8 Block and apply 1 Doubt to all enemies.

bottomless_stamp_pad.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, apply 3 Paperwork to all enemies.

official_timepiece.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 1 Max Energy.

grand_ledger_chain.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 10 Block. Card rewards contain 1 additional card.

charter_fragment.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, apply 2 Doubt and 1 Paperwork to all enemies.

deputy_signature.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 12 Block and apply 2 Paperwork to all enemies.

emergency_inkwell.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 1 Energy.

borrowed_authority.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 1 Strength.

warm_waiting_room_bench.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, heal 3 HP.

forbidden_rubber_stamp.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 1 Strength and 1 Paperwork.

counterfeit_priority_pass.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 1 Energy and 1 Doubt.

official_break_room_key.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, heal 2 HP and gain 4 Block.

overtime_chit.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 1 Energy and 4 Block.

clerical_second_wind.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, heal 5 HP.

municipal_tax_stamp.FlavorText: Gain 5 additional Gold after each victorious combat.

toll_exemption_badge.FlavorText: Gain 10 additional Gold after each victorious combat.

vendor_license.FlavorText: Shop prices are reduced by 15%. When picked up, gain 25 Gold.

brass_filing_tray.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, add a Temporary Authorization to your hand.

deputy_seal.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, apply 1 Paperwork to all enemies and add a Form 12-B to your hand.

queue_bell.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 5 Block and apply 1 Doubt to all enemies.

carbon_copy_pad.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, add a Counter Signature to your hand and a Duplicate Copy to your discard pile.

archive_key.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, add an Archive the Evidence to your hand.

red_tape_dispenser.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, gain 1 Energy and add Red Tape to your hand.

permit_cabinet.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, add an Expedited Stamp to your hand.

bottomless_inbox_tray.FlavorText: At the start of each combat, apply 2 Paperwork to all enemies and add an Unsigned Form to your hand.

paperwork.FlavorText: At the end of the enemy turn, this character loses HP equal to its Paperwork. This ignores Block.

doubt.FlavorText: The next attack made by this character deals 25% less damage. One stack is removed when this triggers.

panic.FlavorText: At the start of the player's turn, draw 1 fewer card. One stack is removed when this triggers.

fatigue.FlavorText: At the start of the player's turn, lose 1 Energy. One stack is removed when this triggers.

strength.FlavorText: This character's attacks deal additional damage equal to its Strength.

poison.FlavorText: At the end of the enemy turn, loses HP equal to Poison, then Poison is reduced by 1.

bureaucrat.FlavorText: Armed with forms, stamps, and a fireproof sense of procedure.
